title: Portable Docker wrapper
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->
Closes #16720.

```release-note
The `docker` wrapper script installed via `make install.docker` now correctly points to the installed `podman` binary.
```

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

comments.body: Changes LGTM, but the tests are not at all happy.  Plus, you need to sign your squished commit. `git commit --amend -s`, then fetch, rebase, and push.

I can do a hacky solution with `sed` if you feel that it's more portable.

Also the CI wants me to write a test, not really sure how to go about that and would appreciate direction.
I don't have a sense for what's best, and I don't have the ability or time to pursue this. Since RHEL has envsubst, I'm fine with your approach. I just want a lot of eyeballs on this.
LGTM

Thanks @multimeric 
You need to add
[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED] to message above to fix Validate
Done. Not sure what's going on with "Cirrus CI / Test build podman-next Copr RPM" though.

Something about `export BINDIR` nags at me, although I can't actually say why. Could you try:
```
    env BINDIR=$BINDIR ETCDIR=$ETCDIR envsubst ....
```
...instead of exporting? 

Also, please don't lose my `/etc/containers/nodocker` comment.
All fixed. I can't decipher the one build failure though.
@lsm5 PTAL

> @lsm5 PTAL

looking

@multimeric if you could apply [this patch](https://paste.centos.org/view/raw/b3c4249a) to your PR, it should resolve the podman-next copr failure. That link will probably die in 24 hours, so I'll post the patch here as well:

```
$ cat 0001-Copr-sanitize-paths-in-usr-bin-docker.patch
From 57b58e0cb431841c77ecbe6a6313ee1cf3b37272 Mon Sep 17 00:00:00 2001
From: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>
Date: Wed, 22 Mar 2023 16:43:01 +0530
Subject: [PATCH] Copr: sanitize paths in /usr/bin/docker

Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>
---
 podman.spec.rpkg | 3 +++
 1 file changed, 3 insertions(+)

diff --git a/podman.spec.rpkg b/podman.spec.rpkg
index 07d0a301b..1d811a6d1 100644
--- a/podman.spec.rpkg
+++ b/podman.spec.rpkg
@@ -176,6 +176,9 @@ PODMAN_VERSION=%{version} %{__make} DESTDIR=%{buildroot} PREFIX=%{_prefix} ETCDI
         install.modules-load
 %endif

+# Sanitize paths in %%{_bindir}/docker
+sed -i 's;%{buildroot}%{_sysconfdir};%{_sysconfdir}/containers;g' %{buildroot}%{_bindir}/docker
+
 install -d -p %{buildroot}/%{_datadir}/%{name}/test/system
 cp -pav test/system %{buildroot}/%{_datadir}/%{name}/test/

--
2.40.0

```
Thanks, I'll apply it when I'm next on my work machine! If you used GitHub suggestions I could apply it straight away but maybe they don't satisfy your commit-signing requirements.

> Thanks, I'll apply it when I'm next on my work machine! If you used GitHub suggestions I could apply it straight away but maybe they don't satisfy your commit-signing requirements.

The changed file in my patch is not included in the PR, so doesn't look like I can add suggestions. Anyway, if you prefer to add those 2 lines to `podman.spec.rpkg` without applying the patch, that's fine too.

I finally, finally understand what's going on. This is the bug: https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/71c5c6efa02386b5d8c627c81d87446fef30d3c4/podman.spec.rpkg#L167

See `ETCDIR`? That should not have `buildroot`! That's a horrible bug, and it is causing this (from [build log](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5655348745338880/logs/main.log)):
```
+ PODMAN_VERSION=0.0.git.18250.0f2910e0
+ /usr/bin/make DESTDIR=/tmp/rpkg/podman-1-nrln4knd/podman-0.0.git.18250.0f2910e0-1.fc37.x86_64 \
    PREFIX=/usr \
    ETCDIR=/tmp/rpkg/podman-1-nrln4knd/podman-0.0.git.18250.0f2910e0-1.fc37.x86_64/etc \
    install.bin install.man install.blah-blah-blah
```
@multimeric could you please, as part of your PR, fix that long-broken specfile?

I am sorry for taking so long to get back to this. Thanks to @lsm5 for the hint that made me see the bug.
LGTM. @lsm5 PTAL
LGTM with caveats mentioned in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18000#discussion_r1155893744

> LGTM with caveats mentioned in [#18000 (comment)](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/18000#discussion_r1155893744)

My caveats were based on old info. Current version works well.

/lgtm
/approve
/hold

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago*, *lsm5*, *multimeric* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago,lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
/hold cancel

The introduction of `gettext-envsubst` package dependency is a show-stopper for RHEL and CentOS - it is not present there. Also usage of envsubst writes the whole path into the docker script which subsequently breaks RPM build and @lsm5 needs to hack it around in https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17791#issuecomment-1479406037

Can we please rethink/revert this?

Probably just `gettext` in RHEL, the `envsubst` split is new IIRC.

Conversation is ongoing right now in #18257, please follow there.

Thanks @edsantiago  - in RHEL envsubst is only part of the gettext package itself.

@lsm5 the BR: gettext-envsubst might be worth conditionalizing in podman.spec.rpkg for fedora only.

> @lsm5 the BR: gettext-envsubst might be worth conditionalizing in podman.spec.rpkg for fedora only.

It's there already. Let me know if I missed anything.

> > @lsm5 the BR: gettext-envsubst might be worth conditionalizing in podman.spec.rpkg for fedora only.
> 
> It's there already. Let me know if I missed anything.

copr builds on `rhcontainerbot/podman-next` have been going fine for quite some time now.

> > > @lsm5 the BR: gettext-envsubst might be worth conditionalizing in podman.spec.rpkg for fedora only.
> > 
> > 
> > It's there already. Let me know if I missed anything.
> 
> copr builds on `rhcontainerbot/podman-next` have been going fine for quite some time now.

https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/rhcontainerbot/podman-next/package/podman/
